channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: LPGA Tour takes center stage in Las Vegas as PGA Tour plots future return
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — Reigning Player of the Year and world No. 1 Nelly Korda is back in the Las Vegas area this week to defend her T-Mobile Match Play title.
item.dc:creator: MARK ANDERSON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Carson Kelly becomes first Cubs player in 32 years to hit for cycle in win over the Athletics
item.description: Chicago Cubs catcher Carson Kelly became the first Cubs player in 32 years to hit for the cycle in the team's 18-3 rout over the Athletics.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 3 Detroit Pistons players suspended for on-court brawl with Timberwolves
item.description: Three Detroit Pistons players have been suspended for an on-court brawl with the Minnesota Timberwolves.
item.dc:creator: Derick Hutchinson, Local 4

item.title: In recruiting battle, lawmakers weigh whether to give college athletes a tax break on NIL earnings
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Illinois state Rep. Travis Weaver remembers how surreal it felt having Heisman Trophy winner Mark Ingram sit a few rows behind him in a political science class. He will never forget the pure joy in Alabama every time the Crimson Tide rolled to the national championship, either.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW SELIGMAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Steelers owner Art Rooney II sees 'positive signs' that Aaron Rodgers will join the team
item.description: Steelers owner Art Rooney II says there are “positive signs” that Aaron Rodgers will play in Pittsburgh.
item.dc:creator: Rob Maaddi, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Red Sox, Garrett Crochet agree to 6-year, $170 million contract extension: report
item.description: The Boston Red Sox and starting pitcher Garrett Crochet reportedly agreed to a six-year, $170 million contract after the team acquired Crochet in the offseason.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Browns' trade for Deshaun Watson was 'big swing and miss,' team owner says
item.description: Cleveland Browns co-owner Jimmy Haslam expressed some remorse for trading for Deshaun Watson and giving him a lucrative extension amid the team's woes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US-Canada-Mexico joint World Cup goes from unity to acrimony thanks to tariffs and '51st state' talk
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Seven years ago, when a joint bid by the United States, Canada and Mexico was awarded the 2026 World Cup, rifts created by tariffs — yes, back then, too! — and a proposed border wall were glossed over because of the neighbors’ longstanding political and economic alliances.
item.dc:creator: HOWARD FENDRICH, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Falcons coach Raheem Morris doesn't rule out trading Kirk Cousins but intends to keep him as backup
item.description: PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — Kirk Cousins has told the Atlanta Falcons he wants to be a starter and coach Raheem Morris didn’t rule out a trade while reiterating the team intends to keep him as the backup to Michael Penix Jr.
item.dc:creator: ROB MAADDI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Athletics bat boy Stewart Thalblum takes down drone in left field
item.description: WEST SACRAMENTO, Calif. (AP) — When a drone suddenly appeared near the left-field wall at Sutter Health Park on Monday night, veteran Athletics bat boy Stewart Thalblum decided to help thwart it.
item.dc:creator: JANIE McCAULEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Carson Kelly becomes 1st Cubs player to hit for the cycle in 32 years in 18-3 rout of A's in West Sacramento
item.description: WEST SACRAMENTO, Calif. (AP) — Carson Kelly had come close to hitting for the cycle, yet that elusive triple kept holding him back. He had just two triples over a 10-year big league career before Monday night. That's when Kelly became the first Cubs player in 32 years to hit for the cycle.
item.dc:creator: JANIE McCAULEY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: A's routed by Cubs 18-3 in Sacramento home opener
item.description: WEST SACRAMENTO, Calif. (AP) -- Carson Kelly hit for the cycle and drove in five runs, Michael Busch launched a two-run homer on the way to a career-high four RBIs, and the Chicago Cubs pounded the Athletics 18-3 on Monday night to spoil their first game in their new Northern California home.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Thunder rout Bulls 145-117 for 10th straight win, improve to 28-1 vs the East
item.description: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander had 27 points and 12 assists in just 27 minutes, and the Oklahoma City Thunder rolled to a 145-117 victory over the Chicago Bulls on Monday night for their 10th straight victory.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Celtics beat Grizzlies 117-103 and complete the first 6-0 trip in franchise history
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. (AP) — Jayson Tatum had 25 points and 14 rebounds, Al Horford scored 26 points and the Boston Celtics swept a six-game road trip for the first time in franchise history with a 117-103 win over the Memphis Grizzlies on Monday night.
item.dc:creator: CLAY BAILEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Curtis Granderson talks Cam Smith and Kyle Tucker, Will Venable and the Sox identity, torpedo bats
item.description: Cubs' top prospect Cam Smith is set to make his MLB debut this season, while the White Sox are off to a respectable start with a 2-2 record, and the Yankees have introduced torpedo bats, which are technically operating within the guidelines of the current rule book but are causing outrage among fans
item.dc:creator: Chris Boden, WGN 9

item.title: Garrett Crochet agrees to $170 million, 6-year deal with Red Sox
item.description: Ace left-hander Garrett Crochet has agreed to a record-breaking $170 million, six-year contract with the Boston Red Sox.
item.dc:creator: Jay Cohen, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Braves' Jurickson Profar gets 80-game PED ban, calls it 'most difficult day' of his career
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Atlanta Braves outfielder Jurickson Profar has been suspended for 80 games without pay for performance-enhancing drug use.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Shower slip knocks Dodgers 1B Freddie Freeman out of lineup after hurting surgically repaired ankle
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Los Angeles Dodgers first baseman Freddie Freeman was out of the lineup Monday night against the Atlanta Braves after slipping in the shower and hurting his surgically repaired right ankle. “He had a little mishap entering the shower,” manager Dave Roberts said.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Milwaukee pays tribute to Bob Uecker in Brewers' first home game since his death
item.description: MILWAUKEE (AP) — As the Milwaukee Brewers conducted a moment of silence before their home opener to honor Bob Uecker, one fan decided keeping quiet wasn’t the best way to pay tribute to the lovable loquacious broadcaster. “We love you, Ueck,” the spectator shouted.
item.dc:creator: STEVE MEGARGEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The NHL and Rogers agree on 12-year, $7.7 US billion media rights deal in Canada, AP source says
item.description: The NHL has reached an agreement with Rogers Communications on a 12-year, $7.7 US billion media rights agreement in Canada, according to a person familiar with the deal. The person spoke to The Associated Press on Monday on condition of anonymity because the move had not been announced.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN WHYNO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Utes lose to Butler in College Basktball Crown
item.description: LAS VEGAS (ABC4 Sports) - The Runnin' Utes got one more opportunity to play this season, but lost to Butler in the inaugural College Basketball Crown tournament in Las Vegas Monday, 86-84. Keanu Dawes, who entered the transfer portal last week, led the Utes with 19 points and 11 rebounds.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Browns owner Jimmy Haslam admits team 'took a big swing and miss' in trading for Deshaun Watson
item.description: PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — Cleveland co-owner Jimmy Haslam admitted that the Browns “took a big swing and miss” with their 2022 trade for quarterback Deshaun Watson. Haslam made the comments on Monday during a session with Browns reporters at the league meetings in Florida.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Dan Mullen, awed by UNLV facilities, says timing felt right to make return to coaching
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — Dan Mullen could've returned to coaching sooner, but the timing never seemed right and, regardless, he was fine remaining at ESPN giving his insights about the latest in college football.
item.dc:creator: MARK ANDERSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ravens president talks Justin Tucker allegations at NFL's annual meeting
item.description: Baltimore Ravens president Sashi Brown spoke briefly about the investigation into the sexual misconduct allegations against kicker Justin Tucker.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Kyren Paris hits solo home run after long weather delay to lift Angels over White Sox 3-2
item.description: Kyren Paris homered on the first pitch he saw after a lengthy rain delay and the Los Angeles Angels rallied to beat the Chicago White Sox 3-2 on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Royals' Jonathan India hit by 99 mph fastball, leaves game
item.description: "He threw a pitch that he doesn't ever throw, the pitch before, which was a four-seam fastball. It was his first one of the year," Pasquantino said.
item.dc:creator: Jared Bush, NewsNation

item.title: Duke's great D sends Blue Devils to their 18th Final Four with an 85-65 win over Alabama
item.description: Duke freshman Cooper Flagg swooped across the paint and swatted an Alabama player’s shot completely off the court. The ball landed in the stands. Flagg and the Blue Devils -- they’re headed to the Final Four.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: USC shakes off JuJu Watkins' absence and beats Kansas State 67-61 in March Madness
item.description: Kennedy Smith stepped into the void left by injured star JuJu Watkins, scoring 19 points to help Southern California hold off Kansas State 67-61 in the Sweet 16 of the women's NCAA Tournament on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: ANNE M. PETERSON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Mavericks outlast Bulls 120-119 to pad lead in play-in race
item.description: Klay Thompson scored 20 points, P.J. Washington had 19 and the Dallas Mavericks beat the Chicago Bulls 120-119 on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Milos Uzan's late bucket on inbound play sends Houston into Elite Eight with 62-60 win over Purdue
item.description: Houston spent time this week practicing an inbound play that coach Kelvin Sampson thought his team might need against Purdue.
item.dc:creator: Michael Marot, Fox 5

item.title: Jazz get blown out by Denver, 129-93
item.description: DENVER (ABC4 Sports) - One more loss by the Utah Jazz will assure the team of its worst record in franchise history, dating all the way back to 1974 in New Orleans.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: 9th-inning homer lifts Rays over Rockies 3-2 in Steinbrenner Field opener
item.description: Rookie Kameron Misner led off the ninth inning with his first major league home run, giving Tampa Bay a 3-2 win over the Colorado Rockies on Friday as the Rays began their season of home games at Steinbrenner Field.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Gianna Kneepkens enters transfer portal
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - Gianna Kneepkens, the leading scorer on the Utah women's basketball team has announced she is entering the transfer portal.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: DePaul women's basketball coach Doug Bruno steps down after 39 seasons
item.description: CHICAGO — Chicago has produced a lot of basketball icons and one of them in Lincoln Park is stepping down — DePaul's longtime women's basketball coach Doug Bruno.
item.dc:creator: Andy Koval, WGN 9

item.title: Opening Day 2025: Cubs have a winning Opening Day record, featuring some memorable moments
item.description: CHICAGO — Opening Day is finally upon us. It's a day that comes with a sense of hope among baseball fans in early spring, a season that represents new beginnings often exemplified by the warmer temperatures and the blooming of trees and flowers.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Illers, WGN 9

item.title: Opening Day 2025: White Sox have a winning Opening Day record, featuring some memorable moments
item.description: CHICAGO — Opening Day is finally upon us. It's a day that comes with a sense of hope among baseball fans in early spring, a season that represents new beginnings often exemplified by the warmer temperatures and the blooming of trees and flowers.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Illers, WGN 9

item.title: Major League Cricket coming to Oakland Coliseum
item.description: OAKLAND, Calif. (KRON) -- In the East Bay, the City of Oakland has announced that Major League Cricket will play matches at the Coliseum this summer. Cricket is the world’s second most popular sport, after soccer.
item.dc:creator: Charles Clifford, KRON4
